title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I think this is going to pair perfectly with our mokkeolli.
I'm going to try it with some sauce because he said all the sauces are house made.
It's going to be our condiments.
This is going to be our raw sabi and our sambi.
So I'm going to boil it and gochujang.

title: will / shall
examples: I think here the food will be amazing but probably a bit more pricey than it is in Seoul.
We'll go again one two.
OK, we'll get one of those, please.
I'll take 100 of these.
It will be in just like a few minutes.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: I think here the food will be amazing but probably a bit more pricey than it is in Seoul.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: We can go each.
Can we get the bill, please?
I can see it.
Can't go wrong with this combo.
Can't go wrong.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: It can't be done in one bite.

title: have to / must
examples: Also, I had to laugh.
I just had to take my jacket off.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: I think the best option would be to get to come and then one tail and one bulgogi.
But I think I would choose bulgogi over tail.
Knowing how much I'd pay for this in Seoul, it's about three times more expensive, at least.
I would say general.
